question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive error handling (onErrorMap(ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException)) to probe the existence and state of topics by observing different gRPC status codes (NOT_FOUND vs INVALID_ARGUMENT) returned for different topicID values, without any signer authorization?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with consensusStartTime set to a future timestamp, and since toFilter() does not validate the caller's identity, subscribe to a topic and receive all messages from that future point onward without the topic owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the missing signer check in subscribeTopic() to subscribe to a governance topic used for DAO voting, receiving all vote messages and potentially correlating voter identities with their votes without the topic owner's authorization?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user receive the running hash chain of a topic via setRunningHash() and setSequenceNumber() in toResponse() without any authorization, allowing them to verify the integrity of the entire message history of a topic they do not own and detect any tampering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of topic-level access control in subscribeTopic() to subscribe to a topic used for private key exchange or secret sharing between applications, receiving all messages without the topic owner's or signers' approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with a topicID that has a very large entity number to toFilter(), causing EntityId.of() to resolve to an unexpected entity, and since no signer check exists, potentially access messages from unintended topics?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the ConsensusController's subscribeTopic() method is a final class method with no security proxy wrapping, meaning Spring Security method-level security annotations cannot intercept it, allowing unauthenticated access to all topics?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a ConsensusTopicResponse with a null runningHash (mapped to ByteString.EMPTY via ProtoUtil.toByteString()) without any signer check, and use the absence of a running hash to infer that a topic message was stored with incomplete data, leaking internal state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the missing authorization in subscribeTopic() to subscribe to a topic used for oracle data feeds, receiving all price or data updates without the oracle operator's approval, enabling front-running or data theft?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with both consensusStartTime and consensusEndTime set to the same value, causing toFilter() to build a filter where startTime equals endTime, and since no signer check exists, probe for the existence of messages at a specific timestamp on any topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive subscription in subscribeTopic() to receive messages from a topic in real time and correlate them with on-chain transactions, effectively deanonymizing transaction participants without the topic owner's or signers' approval?
